description: LTI 1.3 compliant Django app where professors propose projects and students submit ranked preferences; assignments are made by algorithm with professor override, and results are published back to the LMS via AGS.

userStories.title: Django project scaffold
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a working Django project structure so that subsequent stories have a foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Django 5.x project created with a single app named 'projects'
requirements.txt includes: django, django-lti, psycopg2-binary, gunicorn, whitenoise
settings.py reads SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URL, ALLOWED_HOSTS from environment variables
settings.py configures PostgreSQL as the default database
A base HTML template using Bootstrap 5 CDN is present
Running 'python manage.py check' produces no errors
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Database models and migrations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need database models for all core entities so that data can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: Course model: context_id (unique), title, phase (choices: setup/open/closed/assigned/published, default setup), deadline (nullable datetime), lineitem_url (nullable)
Project model: course (FK), title, description, tags (optional text), is_deleted (bool default False)
StudentEnrollment model: course (FK), lti_sub (the LTI sub claim), name (display name) — unique together (course, lti_sub)
Preference model: enrollment (FK), ordered_project_ids (JSON array of project PKs) — one per enrollment
Assignment model: enrollment (OneToOne), project (FK to Project, nullable) — null means Unassigned
Migrations generated and apply cleanly with 'python manage.py migrate'
All models registered in Django admin
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LTI 1.3 endpoints and platform configuration
userStories.description: As an LMS administrator, I want to register the tool in the LMS so that professors and students can launch it.
acceptanceCriteria: django-lti is installed and added to INSTALLED_APPS
LTI urls are included in urls.py (OIDC login initiation, launch redirect, JWKS endpoint)
JWKS endpoint returns the tool's public key at /.well-known/jwks.json
A Platform record can be created via Django admin with: issuer, client_id, auth_login_url, auth_token_url, key_set_url
django-lti cache/nonce backend configured (database cache)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: LTI launch handler with role detection and session
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the LTI launch to identify the user role and store context in the session so that subsequent views know who the user is.
acceptanceCriteria: A launch view handles the LTI 1.3 resource link launch message
On launch, the LTI claims (sub, name, roles, context_id, context_title, lineitem) are stored in the Django session
A Course record is auto-created if one does not exist for the context_id; lineitem_url and title are updated on each launch
A StudentEnrollment record is auto-created for Learner role users (sub + course)
Users with Instructor role are redirected to the professor dashboard
Users with Learner role are redirected to the student view
A decorator or mixin 'lti_required' raises 403 if no valid LTI session exists
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Professor dashboard shell
userStories.description: As a professor, I want to see a course dashboard when I launch the tool so I can manage the project selection process.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /professor/ renders a dashboard page (requires lti_required + Instructor role)
Dashboard shows course title and current phase badge
Dashboard shows the list of projects for the course (title, description, tags)
If no projects exist, an empty state message is shown
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Project CRUD for professors
userStories.description: As a professor, I want to create, edit, and delete projects so that students have a list to choose from.
acceptanceCriteria: Professor can create a project via a form (title required, description required, tags optional)
Professor can edit a project's title, description, and tags when phase is 'setup'
Professor can delete a project when phase is 'setup'; the project is removed from the list
Attempting to delete a project when phase is 'open' or later returns an error message: 'Cannot delete projects after selection has opened'
All project views are scoped to the current course (context_id from session)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Phase transition backend (open, close, deadline, extend)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need backend logic to manage course phase transitions so that the workflow is enforced correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: A service function open_selection(course) transitions phase setup→open; raises ValueError if already past setup
A service function close_selection(course) transitions phase open→closed; raises ValueError if not open
Course.deadline field is set when opening with a deadline; a Django management command or Celery-free approach (checked on each request) auto-closes when deadline has passed
A service function extend_deadline(course, new_deadline) updates the deadline when phase is 'open'; raises ValueError if phase is not open
Phase transitions are enforced: no backward transitions allowed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Phase transition UI on professor dashboard
userStories.description: As a professor, I want action buttons on the dashboard that reflect the current phase so I can advance the workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: When phase is 'setup': show 'Open Selection' button with optional deadline datetime input
When phase is 'open': show 'Close Selection' button and 'Extend Deadline' input (datetime) + submit button
When phase is 'closed': show 'Run Assignment' button (links to assignment view)
When phase is 'assigned': show 'Publish Results' button
When phase is 'published': show a 'Results Published' status message, no further action buttons
Each button POSTs to the appropriate endpoint and redirects back to the dashboard
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Student project list view
userStories.description: As a student, I want to browse the list of available projects so I can make an informed ranking.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /student/ renders project list (requires lti_required + Learner role)
If phase is 'setup' or 'closed': show message 'Selection is not open yet'
If phase is 'open': show all projects as cards with title, description, and status badge (Available or Taken)
A project is 'Taken' if it appears in any Assignment record for this course
If phase is 'assigned': show message 'Assignments are being finalized'
If phase is 'published': redirect to student result view (US-016)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Preference submission backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an endpoint to save a student's ranked project preferences so the algorithm can use them.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /student/preferences/ accepts an ordered list of project PKs
Validates that all submitted project PKs belong to the current course
Validates that at least 3 projects are ranked if more than 3 projects exist; otherwise all projects must be ranked
Validates that the phase is 'open'; returns 400 if not
Creates or updates the Preference record for the student's enrollment
Returns success response (redirect to student view with confirmation message)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preference submission UI
userStories.description: As a student, I want to rank projects by dragging them into order so the system can assign me my best available choice.
acceptanceCriteria: Student project list page (phase open) includes a ranked list section below the project cards
Student can drag projects into a ranked order using SortableJS (included via CDN)
A 'Save Preferences' button submits the ranked order to POST /student/preferences/
If the student has existing preferences, the ranked list is pre-populated in their saved order
A success message is shown after saving: 'Your preferences have been saved'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Assignment algorithm backend
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that assigns projects to students based on preferences so the professor can trigger it.
acceptanceCriteria: A service function run_assignment(course) implements a greedy algorithm: shuffle students randomly (fixed seed from course.context_id for reproducibility), then for each student assign their highest-ranked available project
Students with no preferences are left as Unassigned (Assignment.project = None)
All existing Assignment records for the course are deleted before re-running
Function returns a dict: {enrollment_id: project_id_or_None}
Phase transitions to 'assigned' after algorithm completes
POST /professor/run-assignment/ calls run_assignment(course) and redirects to assignment results page
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Assignment results UI
userStories.description: As a professor, I want to see a summary table of assignments so I can review the results before publishing.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /professor/assignments/ shows a table: Student Name | Assigned Project (or 'Unassigned')
Table is only accessible when phase is 'assigned'
A 'Re-run Assignment' button at the top POSTs to /professor/run-assignment/ and returns to this page
A 'Publish Results' button is shown below the table
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Professor assignment override
userStories.description: As a professor, I want to manually override a student's assigned project so I can correct edge cases before publishing.
acceptanceCriteria: Each row in the assignment table has an 'Override' button
Clicking 'Override' shows a dropdown listing all projects not currently assigned to another student, plus 'Unassigned'
POST /professor/assignments/<enrollment_id>/override/ updates the Assignment record
The previously assigned project is freed (available for override by other students)
The assignment table refreshes showing the updated assignment
Override endpoint returns 403 if phase is not 'assigned'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Publish results and AGS grade passback
userStories.description: As a professor, I want to publish results so students can see their project and the LMS records their grade.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /professor/publish/ transitions phase from 'assigned' to 'published'
For each student with a non-null Assignment, send a score of 1.0 (out of 1.0) to the LMS via AGS using the course's lineitem_url
Students with Assignment.project = None receive no AGS call (LMS grade column stays empty)
If lineitem_url is null, log a warning and skip AGS calls without raising an error
AGS calls use the LTI access token obtained via django-lti's service client
After publish, redirect to the professor dashboard showing 'Results Published' status
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Student published result view
userStories.description: As a student, I want to see my assigned project after results are published so I know what I will be working on.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /student/result/ (or redirect from /student/ when phase is 'published') shows the student's assigned project
If the student has an assignment: show project title, description, and tags
If the student is unassigned: show message 'No project was assigned to you. Please contact your professor.'
Page is only accessible when phase is 'published'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Docker Compose deployment
userStories.description: As a system administrator, I want to deploy the app with docker compose up so it runs in a reproducible environment.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml defines three services: web (Django + Gunicorn), db (PostgreSQL 15), nginx (reverse proxy)
Dockerfile for web service: installs requirements, collects static files, runs gunicorn
nginx.conf proxies / to gunicorn and serves /static/ from the collected static volume
All secrets (SECRET_KEY, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, ALLOWED_HOSTS) are read from a .env file
A .env.example file documents all required variables with placeholder values
.gitignore includes .env
Web container entrypoint runs 'python manage.py migrate' before starting gunicorn
Running 'docker compose up' and visiting http://localhost shows a valid Django response (even if LTI launch is needed to access content)
Typecheck passes
